comment: Data, not code. PRIMARY: the patched+signed falcon APK hosted directly (falcon.apk) — one cross-platform download, no bspatch, no firmware-dump fetch. FALLBACK: reconstruct the byte-identical build from the public Portal firmware dump (stock) + our binary diff (patch) via bspatch. Immortal's provisioner downloads falcon.apk first and only falls back if its URL is blanked. One falcon build serves all Portal families.

apk.note: Primary source: the patched+signed falcon, hosted directly. Cross-platform (Windows has no bspatch) and avoids the flaky firmware-dump fetch. Byte-identical to what the stock+patch reconstruction below produces.

stock.note: Fallback path only: the unmodified stock falcon from the public Portal firmware dump (tadiphone), to which bspatch applies the diff below.

patch.note: Fallback diff: only classes.dex (the 5 code patches) + AndroidManifest.xml differ from stock; all other entries are byte-identical.

result.coexistence_note: Drops the <permission> declarations falcon shares with the Portal+ (aloha) com.amazon.dee.app so both install. This is the A9-validated 'drop-5' build; Alexa revival ships on Android 9 Portals only (A10+ hard-blocks background mic). Standalone (free) falcon is unaffected — same-UID calls don't need them.

millennium.note: Free wake-word build, signed with the dedicated millennium release key (cert sha256 7fe7883592d47c4d55e4f176c52090fca2711c00fc4d53787636a2615b810f42).
